Name: neural_rate_distortion
Title: Exploring Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Compression via Rate-Distortion Theory
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the compression of neural networks can be formulated as a rate-distortion problem, where the goal is to minimize the number of bits required to represent the network while maintaining a certain level of predictive performance. By deriving fundamental limits on the rate-distortion tradeoff, we can better understand the underlying principles of neural network compression and develop more efficient compression algorithms.
Related Work: Previous work has focused on empirical methods for compressing neural networks, such as quantization and pruning. However, these methods lack a clear understanding of the fundamental limits of compression. Our proposal differs from existing work by providing an information-theoretic framework for analyzing neural network compression, which can lead to the development of more efficient and effective compression algorithms.
Abstract: This proposal explores the application of rate-distortion theory to neural network compression. We aim to derive fundamental limits on the rate-distortion tradeoff for neural networks and develop algorithms that achieve these limits. Our approach will provide a theoretical foundation for understanding the compression of neural networks and will lead to the development of more efficient compression methods. We will evaluate our approach using distortion-rate curves and compare our results to existing compression algorithms.
Experiments: Derive a rate-distortion function for neural networks using the information bottleneck method
Develop a compression algorithm that achieves the derived rate-distortion function
Evaluate the performance of the compression algorithm using distortion-rate curves and compare to existing methods
Investigate the effect of different distortion measures on the rate-distortion tradeoff
Risk Factors and Limitations: The derivation of the rate-distortion function may be complex and require significant mathematical analysis
The development of an algorithm that achieves the derived rate-distortion function may be challenging
The evaluation of the compression algorithm may require significant computational resources
